analysis.executive_summary: Audit of Meilisearch documentation (https://www.meilisearch.com/docs) covered 402 pages with 91.5% overall confidence. The site contains 2 confirmed broken internal documentation links requiring immediate repair. API reference coverage stands at 50.0% across 336 detected API pages, indicating significant gaps in usage documentation. Zero percent example reliability and only 5.47% freshness coverage present substantial user trust and maintenance visibility concerns.
strengths: Complete crawl coverage at 100.25% with all 402 discovered pages successfully examined
No repository navigation (GitHub/GitLab) broken links detected; all 2 broken links are user-facing documentation
High overall audit confidence at 91.5% with strong crawl (95%) and link validation (95%) metrics
Single-product topology provides clear documentation structure for users
Low SEO/geo issue rate at 9.02% indicates good technical page optimization
risks: 50% API reference coverage means half of API pages lack adequate usage documentation or examples
0% example reliability estimate suggests code examples are untested, outdated, or unverifiable
Only 5.47% of pages display last-updated timestamps, hiding content freshness from users
2 confirmed broken internal documentation links degrade user navigation and trust
336 API pages detected but only half have sufficient reference coverage creates incomplete developer experience
Lack of freshness metadata prevents users from assessing documentation currency across 94.53% of content
Zero example reliability may lead to user frustration with non-functional code samples

prioritized_actions.action: Repair 2 confirmed broken internal documentation links immediately
Implement last-updated timestamps on all 402 pages to improve freshness visibility from 5.47% to 100%
Establish automated example testing pipeline to validate code samples and raise reliability from 0%
Audit 336 API pages and create usage documentation for the 50% lacking reference coverage
Deploy continuous link monitoring to prevent future broken link accumulation

limitations: External audit cannot verify actual functionality or accuracy of code examples, only detect their presence and structure
API coverage detection relies on page structure patterns; actual completeness of API documentation requires domain expertise review
Crawl cannot assess content quality, technical accuracy, or alignment with current product version
Link validation confirms HTTP responses but cannot verify contextual appropriateness of link destinations
Freshness detection depends on visible metadata; actual content currency may differ from displayed timestamps
